item.title: spitr 0.5.5
item.description:
feat(dmg): plain legible installer window (drop custom background)
Finder draws DMG icon labels in the OS appearance color, which a disk image can't override — so a custom background is only legible in one of light/dark mode (dark-on-dark in Light mode with our dark art). Switch to a clean icon-view window (app left, Applications right, no background), legible in both appearances. Removes the background art + generator.
Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

item.title: spitr 0.5.4
item.description:
feat(dmg): styled installer window (on-brand background + layout)
make-dmg.sh now stages a writable image, sets an icon-view Finder window with the committed Meridian background (dark graphite, Orbitron wordmark, lemon drag arrow), positions the app and Applications symlink, then converts to compressed read-only. Background is pre-rendered by make-dmg-bg.swift and committed so the release pipeline renders nothing.
Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

item.title: spitr 0.5.3
item.description:
fix: load fonts via Bundle.main, not Bundle.module (relocatable builds)
SwiftPM's Bundle.module accessor bakes in an absolute .build/release path and fatal-errors at launch when that path is absent. Local builds happened to find it (the path exists on the build machine), masking the bug — but any relocated build (CI, another Mac, the installed DMG on a fresh machine) crashed in Theme.registerFonts at startup.
Fonts now live in the app bundle's Resources/Fonts and load via Bundle.main; Bundle.module is only a dev fallback for `swift run`. bundle.sh copies the .ttf files there directly and no longer ships the SwiftPM resource bundle. Verified: the app launches with .build removed.
Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

item.title: spitr 0.5.0
item.description: Auto-updates (Sparkle), in-app Settings, transcript history, frosted-glass HUD with hands-free dictation and TTS controls, and stability fixes.
